heading: Default Alert Settings
body:
Atlas provides the following default alerts and alert values for a new
project:

- [Cluster is missing an active mongos](<Cluster is missing an active mongos>)

- [Connections % of configured limit](<Connections % of configured limit is>), `above 80`

- [Credit card is about to expire](<Credit card is about to expire>)

- [Disk space % used on Data Partition](<Disk space % used on Data Partition is>), `above 90`

- [Host has index suggestions](<Host has index suggestions>)

- [Query Targeting: Scanned Objects / Returned](<Query Targeting: Scanned Objects / Returned>), `above 1000`

  > **Note:** The default configuration sends an alert only if the
  > ratio of documents scanned to documents returned meets or
  > exceeds the specified threshold for at least 10 minutes.

- [Replica set has no primary](<Replica set has no primary>)

- [Replication Oplog Window is (X)](<Replication Oplog Window is>), `below 1 hours`

- [System: CPU (User) % is](<System: CPU (User) % is>), `above 95`

- [Total Namespaces is](<Total Namespaces is>), `above 10000`

- [User joined the project](<User joined the project>)

Atlas provides the following default alert for projects
using [Amazon Web Services Key Management Service](security-aws-kms):

- [alert](<AWS encryption key elapsed time since last rotation is above (n) days>)

Atlas provides the following default MongoDB Search alerts:

- [Atlas Search: Max Number of Fields Indexed is](<Atlas Search: Max Number of Fields Indexed is>)

- [Atlas Search: Max Number of nGram Fields Indexed is](<Atlas Search: Max Number of nGram Fields Indexed is>)

- [Atlas Search: Max Number of Lucene Docs](<Atlas Search: Max Number of Lucene Docs is>)

- [Insufficient disk space to support rebuilding search indexes](<Insufficient disk space to support rebuilding search indexes>)

- [Atlas Search: Mongot stopped replication](<Atlas Search: Mongot stopped replication>)

- [Search Process: Ran out of memory](<Search Process: Ran out of memory>)

- [Atlas Search: Mongot paused initial sync](<Atlas Search: Mongot paused initial sync>)

- [Atlas Search: Mongot is approaching replication stop threshold](<Atlas Search: Mongot is approaching replication stop threshold>)

For a full list of MongoDB Search alerts, see [MongoDB Search alerts](alert-conditions-atlas-search).

Atlas provides the following default Atlas Stream Processing alerts:

- [Stream Processor State is failed](<Stream Processor State is failed>)

Atlas provides default alerts for projects with clusters that experience
auto-scaling events. To learn more about all auto-scaling alert events, see
[Atlas autoscaling alerts](alert-conditions-autoscaling).

Atlas provides the following default Atlas Stream Processing alerts:

- [Stream Processor State is failed](<Stream Processor State is failed>)

Atlas provides default alerts for projects with clusters that experience
auto-scaling events. To learn more about all auto-scaling alert events, see
[Atlas autoscaling alerts](alert-conditions-autoscaling).

Atlas provides the following default alerts for organizations that
use [Service Account](service-accounts-overview).

- [Service Account Secrets are about to expire](<Service Account Secrets are about to expire>)

- [Service Account Secrets have expired](<Service Account Secrets have expired>)

Atlas provides the following default alerts for organizations that
use [Service Account](service-accounts-overview).

- [Service Account Secrets are about to expire](<Service Account Secrets are about to expire>)

- [Service Account Secrets have expired](<Service Account Secrets have expired>)
